LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR B&P 17200 UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT PLEAD AROUND THE ABSOLUTE BAR TO RELIEF CREATED BY INSURANCE CODE SECTION 790.03 BY RECASTING THE CAUSE OF ACTION AS ONE FOR UNFAIR COMPETITION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD FRAUD WITH SPECIFICITY. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR, WITHOUT PREJUDICE IN VIEW OF COURT'S RULING ON DEMURRER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD WITH SPECIFICITY HOW PLAINTIFF HAS MET THE REQUIREMENTS OF INSURANCE CODE SECTION 11580.2(I). INSUFFICIENT FACTS REGARDING BODILY INJURY SUIT FILED AGINST UNINSURED MOTORIST OR WAIVER OR ESTOPPEL. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION PLEADS FACTS THAT ESSENTIALLY AMOUNT TO BAD FAITH CLAIMS THAT ARE SUBJECT TO INSURANCE CODE SECTION 790.03. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT PLEAD AROUND THE ABSOLUTE BAR TO RELIEF CREATED BY THAT STATUTE BY RECASTING THE CAUSE OF ACTION AS ONE FOR UNFAIR COMPETITION. PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD WITH SPECIFICITY: WHO, WHAT, WHERE, WHEN, AND WHY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ESURANCE, INCORPORATED DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PLEAD THE REQUIREMENTS OF INS. CODE SECTION 11580.2(I) TO SUPPORT BREACH OF CONTRACT. DEFENDANT MAY CONDUCT DISCOVERY, AND PLAINTIFF HAS THE AFFIRMATIVE DUTY TO COMMENCE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. NO BAD FAITH CLAIM SURVIVES WITHOUT BREACH OF CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT PLEAD AROUND THE ABSOLUTE BAR TO RELIEF CREATED BY INS. CODE SECTION 11580.2(I) BY RECASTING THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION AS ONE FOR UNFAIR COMPETITION. CONDUCT DESCRIBED IS PROHIBITED BY INS. CODE SECTION 790.3. FRAUD IS NOT PLED WITH THE REQUISITE PARTICULARITY. DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF WILL REDRAFT ITS COMPLAINT AND FILE AN AMENDED PLEADING. NO ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTION ALLOWED, WITHOUT COURT APPROVAL. (DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN ALL RESPECTS EXCEPT OVERRULED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 21, 22, 29 AND 30. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION, 302 DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PARAGRAPH 11-17 AND PARAGRAPH 34, SENTENCE RELATED TO WRONGFUL DENIAL OF BENEFITS, WHICH ARE IRRELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION. MOTION GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND REGARDING LOST EARNINGS IF PLAINTIFF CAN FACTUALLY SUPPORT IT. MOTION DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGE ALLEGATIONS AND PREJUDGMENT INTEREST AS PROVIDED BY LAW. (DEFENDANT TO PREPARE PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD SPECIFIC ACTS BY DEFENDANT THAT AMOUNT TO OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AND PLEAD FACTS DESCRIBING THE SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. (DEFENDANT TO PREPARE PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.) MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD SPECIFIC ACTS BY DEFENDANT THAT AMOUNT TO OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AND PLEAD FACTS DESCRIBING THE SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. (Defendant to prepare proposed form of order.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ESURANCE, INCORPORATED DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Amy Goltz, Esq. Law Office of Scott L. Woodall (925) 600-0111 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Christiane Sinclair, Esq. of Coddington, Hicks & Danforth (650) 592-5400 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 4th causes of action. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. Plaintiff fails to plead the requirements of Ins. Code section 11580.2(i) to support breach of contract. Defendant may conduct discovery, and plaintiff has the affirmative duty to commence arbitration proceedings. No bad faith claim survives without breach of contract. Plaintiff may not plead around the absolute bar to relief created by Ins. Code section 11580.2(i) by recasting the 3rd cause of action as one for unfair competition. Conduct described is prohibited by Ins. Code section 790.3. Fraud is not pled with the requisite particularity. Demurrer to the 6th Cause of action is granted with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff will redraft its Complaint and file an amended pleading. No additional causes of action allowed, without Court approval. (Defense counsel will prepare a proposed Order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Hearing On Deft Demurrer To Pltf'S First Amended Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED Demurrer To Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer as to the first, second, third and fourth causes of action sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to the first and second causes of action, plaintiff must plead with specificity how plaintiff has met the requirements of Insurance Code section 11580.2(i). Insufficient facts regarding bodily injury suit filed aginst uninsured motorist or waiver or estoppel. The third cause of action pleads facts that essentially amount to bad faith claims that are subject to Insurance Code section 790.03. Plaintiff may not plead around the absolute bar to relief created by that statute by recasting the cause of action as one for unfair competition. Plaintiff to plead fourth cause of action for fraud with specificity: who, what, where, when, and why. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
